```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
set.seed(sample.int(.Machine$integer.max, 1))

## Parametros
theta_hat <- round(runif(1, 2, 8), 2)
theta0    <- sample(2:7, 1)
# garantir que theta_hat != theta0
while (abs(theta_hat - theta0) < 0.5) {
  theta_hat <- round(runif(1, 2, 8), 2)
  theta0    <- sample(2:7, 1)
}
se_hat <- round(runif(1, 0.3, 1.5), 2)

## Estatistica de Wald: W = (theta_hat - theta0)^2 / se_hat^2
W <- round((theta_hat - theta0)^2 / se_hat^2, 4)

## Respostas
correct <- W
w1 <- round((theta_hat - theta0) / se_hat, 4)         # Z (nao W)
w2 <- round(W + 1, 4)
w3 <- round(abs(theta_hat - theta0) / se_hat^2, 4)    # sem quadrado no numerador
w4 <- round((theta_hat - theta0)^2 / se_hat, 4)       # sem quadrado no SE

all_vals <- c(correct, w1, w2, w3, w4)
# Garante cinco alternativas numericamente distintas após a parametrização.
integer_choices <- all(abs(all_vals - round(all_vals)) < sqrt(.Machine$double.eps))
choice_step <- if (integer_choices) 1 else max(1e-4, max(abs(all_vals), na.rm = TRUE) * 0.01)
while (anyDuplicated(all_vals)) {
  duplicate_position <- which(duplicated(all_vals))[1]
  all_vals[duplicate_position] <- max(all_vals, na.rm = TRUE) + choice_step
}
choices <- sample(all_vals)
sol_pos <- which(choices == correct)
sol_str <- paste(as.integer(seq_along(choices) == sol_pos), collapse = "")
```

Question
========
O teste de Wald para $H_0:\theta = `r theta0`$ usa a estatistica $W = (\hat{\theta} - \theta_0)^2/\widehat{\text{Var}}(\hat{\theta})$. Com $\hat{\theta} = `r theta_hat`$, $\theta_0 = `r theta0`$ e erro padrao estimado $\widehat{\text{EP}}(\hat{\theta}) = `r se_hat`$ (portanto $\widehat{\text{Var}}(\hat{\theta}) = `r se_hat`^2 = `r round(se_hat^2,4)`$), o valor de $W$ e:

Answerlist
----------
* `r choices[1]`
* `r choices[2]`
* `r choices[3]`
* `r choices[4]`
* `r choices[5]`

Solution
========
$W = \frac{(\hat{\theta} - \theta_0)^2}{\widehat{\text{Var}}(\hat{\theta})} = \frac{(`r theta_hat` - `r theta0`)^2}{`r se_hat`^2} = \frac{`r round((theta_hat-theta0)^2,4)`}{`r round(se_hat^2,4)`} = `r W`$.

Sob $H_0$, $W \xrightarrow{D} \chi^2_1$. Note que $\sqrt{W} = Z = (\hat{\theta}-\theta_0)/\widehat{\text{EP}}(\hat{\theta})$ e a estatistica $z$ de Wald.

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap6S5Q9_teste_Wald
extype: schoice
exsolution: `r sol_str`
exshuffle: FALSE
